conventions: Each task is independently implementable once its dependsOn tasks are done. Every code task ends green on `deno task check` and `deno task lint`; tasks with tests also end green on `deno task test`. Sizes: S = well under an hour, M = an hour or two.

tasks.title: Add MCP SDK dependency and publish exclude
tasks.description: Add "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "npm:@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@^1.29.0" to imports. Add "**/testdata/" to publish.exclude (fixture server lands in mcp-09).
acceptance: deno task check passes
deno info npm:@modelcontextprotocol/sdk resolves to a 1.29.x version

tasks.title: Internal MCP contract types (types.ts)
tasks.description: Define the SDK-free internal contract: McpToolDef { name, description?, inputSchema: JSONSchema }; McpContentBlock (text | image | audio | resource_link | embedded resource, with mimeType/data/uri fields per spec); McpCallResult { content, structuredContent?, isError? }; McpClient { listTools, callTool(name, args, timeout?), close }; McpStdioTransportOptions { command, args?, env?, cwd? }; McpHttpTransportOptions { url, headers? }; structural McpTransport { start, send, close, ... } matching the SDK Transport shape without importing it; McpTransportOptions union of all three. JSONSchema type comes from @huuma/validate.
acceptance: No import of @modelcontextprotocol/sdk anywhere in the file
deno task check passes

tasks.title: PassthroughSchema (schema.ts)
tasks.description: Implement PassthroughSchema implementing Schema<Record<string, unknown>> from @huuma/validate (interface: validate, infer, jsonSchema(), isRequired() — see @huuma/validate schema.ts:34). Constructor takes the server's raw JSON Schema. validate() passes any value through as successful validation, coercing null/undefined to {}; jsonSchema() returns the stored schema verbatim; isRequired() returns true. Not exported from the tools barrel.
acceptance: Tests: jsonSchema() returns the exact object passed in; validate({a:1}) yields value {a:1} with errors undefined; validate(undefined) and validate(null) yield {}; a PassthroughSchema instance is assignable where Tool's input is expected (compile-time check in test)
deno task test green

tasks.title: Tool naming: validation, sanitize, prefix, cap (naming.ts)
tasks.description: validateServerName(name): throw unless /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/. modelToolName(server, tool): sanitize tool chars outside [A-Za-z0-9_-] to '_' (MCP allows '.', providers don't), build `${server}_${tool}`; if result exceeds 64 chars, truncate to 59 and append '_' + 4-hex djb2 hash of the full unsanitized combined name. Result always matches /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$/. Deterministic: same inputs, same output.
acceptance: Tests: plain prefix; '.'-containing tool name sanitized; combined name > 64 chars capped to exactly 64 and regex-valid; two long names identical up to the truncation point yield different results; determinism across calls; validateServerName rejects 'my server' and 'srv.1', accepts 'github' and 'my-srv_2'
deno task test green

tasks.title: Result flattening (content.ts)
tasks.description: flattenResult(result: McpCallResult): string. Order: (1) isError true -> throw Error with text blocks joined by '\n', or 'MCP tool call failed' when no text; (2) structuredContent present -> JSON.stringify(structuredContent), ignore duplicate text blocks; (3) otherwise map blocks: text -> text, image -> `[image ${mimeType}]`, audio -> `[audio ${mimeType}]`, resource_link/resource -> `[resource ${uri}]`, join with '\n'; empty content -> ''.
acceptance: Tests cover each branch: text join, structuredContent preference over text, image/audio/resource placeholders, isError throw with joined text, isError throw with fallback message, empty content
deno task test green

tasks.title: SDK seam: connect() (client.ts)
tasks.description: The only file importing @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. connect(options: McpTransportOptions): Promise<McpClient>. Transport selection by shape: 'command' in options -> StdioClientTransport; 'url' in options -> StreamableHTTPClientTransport; object with start/send/close functions -> used as-is. new Client({ name: '@huuma/ai', version }) from client/index.js, await client.connect(transport). Returned wrapper maps SDK results onto types.ts shapes: listTools() -> McpToolDef[] (inputSchema cast to JSONSchema); callTool(name, args, timeout?) -> McpCallResult, forwarding timeout as SDK RequestOptions when set; close() delegates. No SDK type appears in any exported signature.
acceptance: grep for '@modelcontextprotocol' across src/ matches only client.ts
Exported signatures reference only types.ts types
deno task check and lint pass (behavior is covered by mcp-10/mcp-11)

tasks.title: mcp() factory and McpConnection handle (mcp.ts)
tasks.description: Public surface. McpToolsOptions { name, transport: McpTransportOptions, allowedTools?: string[], timeout?: number }. mcp(options): validateServerName, connect(), initial listTools, return McpConnection. McpConnection: tools(): Tool<any, string>[] returning a fresh array copy of the cached tools; refresh(): re-list and rebuild the cache (documented: already-constructed agents keep their snapshot); close(): delegate to McpClient. Tool building: filter defs by allowedTools (original names); each def -> new Tool({ name: modelToolName(options.name, def.name), description: def.description ?? '', input: new PassthroughSchema(def.inputSchema), fn: async (props) => flattenResult(await client.callTool(def.name, props, options.timeout)) }). No try/catch in fn (ADR 0001: errors propagate). JSDoc @example mirroring subagent.ts style, including the close() obligation and Deno permissions (stdio: --allow-run --allow-read --allow-env; HTTP: --allow-net).
acceptance: deno task check and lint pass
The fn closure demonstrably calls the server with the original (unprefixed) tool name — asserted later in mcp-10 case 2
Docblock example present and consistent with README example added in mcp-12

tasks.title: Re-export from tools barrel
tasks.description: Add alongside the other factory re-exports: export { mcp, McpConnection, type McpToolsOptions } from '@/tools/mcp/mcp.ts'; also re-export type McpTransportOptions (and the stdio/http option types) so users can type configs. Internal modules (types/client/schema/naming/content) stay unexported.
acceptance: import { mcp } from the tools barrel type-checks
deno task check passes; barrel exposes no SDK or internal-module types

tasks.title: stdio test fixture server (testdata/server.ts)
tasks.description: Minimal stdio MCP server on the SDK server API (dev-only, excluded from publish via the testdata exclude from mcp-01; deliberately not named *_test.ts so the test runner never executes it). Tools: add {a,b} -> text sum; echo_json {value} -> structuredContent { value } plus a duplicate text block; fail {} -> isError true with a text message. Connect via StdioServerTransport at top level.
acceptance: deno task check passes
Manually verified once: deno run --allow-read --allow-env testdata/server.ts starts and answers an initialize request (automated coverage lands in mcp-11)

tasks.title: In-memory integration tests (mcp.test.ts)
tasks.description: Real SDK McpServer in-process, wired via InMemoryTransport.createLinkedPair(); client side passed through the custom-transport escape hatch (dev-only SDK import in the test file is acceptable — publish excludes tests). Cases: (1) lists and wraps: names are `${server}_${tool}`, tool.input.jsonSchema() returns the server schema unchanged; (2) server receives the original unprefixed name; (3) structuredContent preferred over duplicate text; (4) multi-content flattening incl. image placeholder; (5) isError rejects, and via callTool() lands in { result: { error } }; (6) allowedTools filters by original name; (7) >64-char combined name capped and regex-valid end-to-end; (8) invalid server name throws at factory time before connecting; (9) refresh() picks up a newly registered server tool, previously returned arrays unchanged; (10) agent end-to-end with the StubModel pattern from agent/mod.test.ts scripted to call an MCP tool through Agent.run.
acceptance: All 10 cases green under deno task test
No child processes or network used

tasks.title: stdio child-process integration test (mcp_stdio.test.ts)
tasks.description: Guard the Deno node-compat path (the SDK's least-tested surface). mcp({ name: 'fixture', transport: { command: Deno.execPath(), args: ['run', '--allow-read', '--allow-env', <fixture path resolved from import.meta>] } }); assert tools listed, fixture_add call returns the sum, close() resolves promptly (child reaped, no test-runner leak warnings — Deno's sanitizers fail the test on leaked child processes).
acceptance: Test green under deno task test (flags already grant --allow-run)
No resource/op sanitizer failures

tasks.title: README: MCP section and permissions
tasks.description: Extend 'What is included' with MCP server tools. Add a short example (API frozen by ADR 0002): await mcp({ name, transport }) -> spread .tools() into agent() -> await close(). Extend the Permissions section: stdio -> --allow-run --allow-read --allow-env; Streamable HTTP -> --allow-net. Can be written before the code exists; final wording verified against the shipped API in mcp-13.
acceptance: Example matches the ADR 0002 API sketch exactly
Permissions section lists both transport permission sets

tasks.title: Validation sweep
tasks.description: Full gate: deno task check; deno task lint; deno task test (new suites plus existing); deno task publish:dry-run. Confirm the dry run ships mcp.ts/client.ts/types.ts/schema.ts/naming.ts/content.ts under ./tools and excludes testdata/ and all test files. Verify the README example against the shipped API (aligns mcp-12 with mcp-07's docblock).
acceptance: All four tasks green
Publish file list contains the six mcp source files and no testdata/test files
